summary: Red-review of the proposed evidence-use guard for qv6_007-style failures. The guard is worth pursuing only as a narrow diagnostic/reporting gate for source-supported-but-underanswered physical access-control evidence, with explicit source/retrieval scope and false-positive fixtures before any broad paid loop reopens.

models.review_method: Read-only local artifact/source inspection and proposal writing; no generator, retriever, judge, API, or model calls were run.

outputs.description: Contract output for the L48 evidence-use guard red review.
Human-readable red review of overfitting risks, false-positive cases, labels, and proof bar.

proposal.bottom_line: Do not implement a broad generator-time rule that forces confirmation whenever any access-control marker appears. Implement, if reopened, a narrow no-API evidence-use diagnostic that fires only when target-scoped retrieved/source evidence supports physical access-control inclusion and the generated answer denies or cannot-confirm that inclusion.

accepted_risks.risk: A qv6_007-specific guard can overfit to an exposed case.
accepted_risks.why_acceptable: Acceptable only if labeled diagnostic_only_exposed_case and not counted as EDD, generalization, holdout, or system-wide claim-preservation evidence.

accepted_risks.risk: Literal markers are brittle under mojibake, spacing, and paraphrase.
accepted_risks.why_acceptable: Acceptable for no-API triage if marker hits are reported as evidence, not hidden authority, and a fixture pack covers negation, false friends, missing source, and source leakage.

accepted_risks.risk: A failed guard can block report-ready promotion even when judge scores are high.
accepted_risks.why_acceptable: L44 shows judge 5/5 while the physical access-control claim is still source_supported_but_answer_underanswered; the reporting caveat is the point of the guard.

rejected_risks.risk: Treating any access-control term in retrieved/source context as proof that the answer must confirm physical access-control inclusion.
rejected_risks.reason: The codebase already distinguishes physical access-control from system/account/network access false friends; a keyword-only rule would create false positives.

rejected_risks.risk: Using whole-file source hits from data_list.csv as support for the target case.
rejected_risks.reason: scripts/check_claim_preservation.py can gather text from matching source files/rows; future guard proof should bind hits to retrieved chunks or project-scoped rows before blaming generation.

rejected_risks.risk: Calling a failure evidence-use when target evidence was not retrieved or retrieval scope was mixed/off-target.
rejected_risks.reason: src/retriever.py can auto-filter, MMR, rerank, and ensure org coverage. Guard labels must separate target_missing/mixed_org retrieval failures from generation evidence-use failures.

rejected_risks.risk: Passing an answer merely because it repeats the physical access-control marker while saying it cannot be confirmed.
rejected_risks.reason: Existing L47 logic correctly gives underanswer polarity priority over marker presence.

rejected_risks.risk: Promoting the qv6_007 two-claim diagnostic into an EDD or system-wide preservation score.
rejected_risks.reason: The L47 report explicitly labels the gate diagnostic_only_exposed_case; broad paid runs remain closed.

false_positive_cases_to_require.setup: Retrieved/source context and answer mention access permissions, account roles, connection logs, server/network access control, or security products, but no physical entry/exit access-control purchase/install evidence.
Answer confirms physical access-control purchase/install inclusion but says RFID/card/biometric/vendor/interface details are not confirmed.
The physical access-control marker appears in another organization or another Goyang-like project, while qv6_007 retrieved chunks do not contain target-bound support.
Answer cannot confirm physical access control because retrieved_orgs lacks the target Goyang organization.
Retrieved context includes target and non-target facility records with access-control language.
Answer repeats the access-control-system marker in a sentence saying it is not confirmed.
Source supports purchase/install but answer says the physical access-control system is absent or not included.
Source and retrieval support the physical access-control claim but answer omits it entirely.

allowed_summary_language: The guard catches a qv6_007-style residual evidence-use failure when target-scoped physical access-control evidence is available but the answer says it cannot be confirmed.
The result is diagnostic-only and blocks report-ready promotion for this exposed case.
Broad paid loops remain closed until false-positive fixtures and target-scoped proof pass locally.
disallowed_summary_language: any access-control evidence should always be confirmed

proof_required_before_reopening_broad_pa: A no-API fixture pack with at least the false-positive cases listed here, including false friends, narrow unknown detail boundary, wrong-project leakage, target-missing retrieval, mixed-org retrieval, negated marker echo, and source-supported omission.

A same-cohort qv6_007 rerun or saved-detail replay where retrieved_orgs is target_only, physical access-control source support is bound to retrieved chunk IDs or project-scoped source rows, and the answer confirms inclusion while keeping unknown details narrow.
A parsed guard report showing zero critical failures, no source_scope_unbound_or_leaked cases, and explicit diagnostic_only_exposed_case/not_edd_scoreboard labels.
A regression check proving the guard does not convert system/account/network access-control language into physical access-control support.
A reporting/aggregation check proving claim-guard artifacts do not enter EDD scoreboard rows and that judge 5/5 cannot override a critical claim-gate failure.
Cost/budget dry-run evidence showing the next loop would run the frozen cohort intended, with no broad paid expansion triggered by a diagnostic-only pass alone.
implementation_guidance_if_reopened: Prefer an offline post-generation/report gate over a generator prompt mutation until false positives are cleared.
Require target-bound evidence: retrieved chunk IDs, project-scoped CSV rows, or source spans matching target_org and project terms.
Evaluate retrieval_scope_status before generation labels; do not blame generation when target evidence was absent or ambiguous.
Use polarity-aware sentence/bullet checks so 'inclusion confirmed, method unknown' passes while 'inclusion cannot be confirmed' fails.
Keep false_friend_hits status-affecting for this claim when no physical access-control counter-marker is present.

commands_run: Select-String -LiteralPath 'eval/parallel_runs/20260706_230033_EDD-loop-under-10-USD-with-recorded-cause-result-insight/worker_outputs/l48_guard_red_review.md' -Pattern 'Proof Before Broad Paid Loops','No API/model calls were made'
observed_results: No paid API/model calls were made.
The workspace path I:/0706/rfp-rag-project is not a git repository, so git status could not provide dirty-worktree verification.
src/generator.py builds prompts and calls OpenAI for generation; this review did not invoke it.
src/retriever.py can auto-filter orgs, retrieve with MMR/rerank, and preserve multi-org coverage; guard labels must account for retrieval scope.
scripts/check_claim_preservation.py is a local deterministic checker and currently uses marker, underanswer, false-friend, and retrieval-scope fields.
L47 report says the existing gate is diagnostic_only_exposed_case and broad paid v7/v8/v9 runs remain closed.

L44 claim-preservation result shows target_only retrieval, 1/2 claims passed, physical_access_control_system failed as source_supported_but_answer_underanswered, while the saved L44 judge score was 5/5.
Prior L47 red/schema outputs already warn about source-scope false positives, false friends, polarity, and overclaiming qv6_007 diagnostics.
The L48 contract parsed as JSON with schema=parallel_team_worker_output.v1 and task_id=l48_guard_red_review.
Both allowed L48 output files exist under worker_outputs.
The markdown output contains the required proof-before-paid-loops section and no-API verification note.
